description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metal frame. The solar panel array is elevated above a patterned base platform, supported by angled metal struts that provide stability and clearance beneath the panels. The base platform displays a geometric, high-contrast design in shades of gray, black, and blue, likely for visual distinction or anti-slip purposes. The solar panels themselves are composed of dark, grid-patterned photovoltaic cells, typical of modern solar technology. The mounting structure appears to be made of metal, likely steel or aluminum, and is designed for ground installation. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are apparent in the images, and the array is configured as a single, continuous row of panels.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array on a patterned platform, supported by angled metal struts and featuring a large, dark photovoltaic surface.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar panel array and the angled support struts on both sides.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the width of the panel array and the geometric pattern on the base platform.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the solar panel array and the supporting structure, with the patterned base clearly visible.
description_view_3: Reverse end view focusing on the back of the panel array and the contrasting design of the base platform.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum frame
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 8-10 panels
